student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Comparing places, habits, and options with comparatives and superlatives -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- Comparing Things
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 7 -- Comparing Things

listenings.text: I have lived in two very different cities, and people often ask me to compare them. My first city was bigger and more exciting, with more restaurants and a much busier nightlife. It was never boring. But it was also more expensive and a lot more crowded, and the traffic was terrible. My second city is smaller and quieter. Life there is more affordable, the air is cleaner, and my commute is much shorter. Of course, it has fewer options for going out on the weekend. Honestly, the biggest advantage of the small city is the peace. For me, the quieter city is the better place to live now, but the busy city was the more exciting one when I was younger.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! You asked me to help you choose a gym, so I visited two of them yesterday. The first gym is closer to your apartment and it is more affordable, but it is older and the machines are not as modern. The second gym is bigger and much more modern, with a pool and newer equipment, but it is more expensive and a little farther away. In my opinion, the second one is the best option if you want the pool, but the first one is more convenient for a busy week. They are actually more similar than you think. Call me back and tell me which one you prefer. Bye!

extra_audio.text: Name the two options you want to compare. Compare their price with a comparative like cheaper or more expensive. Compare another feature, like the size or the location. Say which option is the best and explain why. Say which one you prefer and give your final choice.
